title: Upon The Hill And Grove At Bill-borow by Andrew Marvell — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
To the Lord Fairfax.

See how the arched Earth does here
Rise in a perfect Hemisphere!
The stiffest Compass could not strike
A line more circular and like;
Nor softest Pensel draw a Brow.
So equal as this Hill does bow.
meaning: "Upon The Hill And Grove At Bill-borow" by Andrew Marvell is a sad and love and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 91 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "To the Lord Fairfax. ...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Andrew Marvell's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
